subject: Blueberry or chocolate chip?
disclaimer_text: Paid for by Trump Make America Great Again Committee, a joint fundraising committee authorized by and composed of Donald J
body:
Bacon. Pancakes. President Trump. |
---
| | | | | | | |

Peter,

President Trump's first year in office is quickly drawing to a close, and he wants to finish the way he started - talking to the REAL Americans who've stood by his side.

On December 2nd, the President is hosting a very special Christmas Breakfast and is inviting one lucky winner to join him. He wanted to make sure EVERY loyal supporter across the nation got the word.
[](http://click.campaigns.rnchq.com/?qs=f8a114297104500b524ce681a331c315e4ca149ad728dc49e0447752555182854bbf696019524a17b3f0704eea633557)**[All he's asking is that you make a contribution of $3 by November 27 to help support our America First agenda.](http://click.campaigns.rnchq.com/?qs=f8a114297104500b3cdcb0348afe749298725bde18158f759434a16eceee5bd2c7a87f3b53992931257d0da3a166123b)**
**[ENTER TO WIN](http://click.campaigns.rnchq.com/?qs=f8a114297104500bdb79761a87b806b91972c73df68795c12d5d638285477e7fd34a02ef8c69eec7af979d46c4292079)**
One day, you'll be telling your grandchildren about the time you had pancakes and bacon with President Trump.

Don't miss out. **[Enter to win before the deadline.](http://click.campaigns.rnchq.com/?qs=f8a114297104500b65b968a5ea85cca5f2b8edcb4e234f584f50e17a3de3814ea22704e1c7d9813348bb77979fa8ca2b)**

Best of luck,

Team TRUMP **[ENTER TO WIN](http://click.campaigns.rnchq.com/?qs=f8a114297104500b9fb929a6e9b03be3e1f98cfa879ecb773ec8f31393056a5cdd5e57109706c481d7e29ed15fe8e968)**

**\---------- Forwarded Message ----------**
**From:** Donald J. Trump
**Date:** November 10, 2017
**Subject:** Meet me for breakfast, Peter
**To:** **[dpwillis67@gmail.com](http://click.campaigns.rnchq.com/?qs=f8a114297104500b3708ba6d83252697b47545cdc3baf3301119c09b40f8200bcd32840464156d436aa4370d968ed73b)**

---
| [](http://click.campaigns.rnchq.com/?qs=f8a114297104500b690df9b7cbb979956b18a0a5e5ae85b1de4c5946b717aa9a8c2540f751e01c43d736869491e73409)
---

Peter,

I've been in office for almost one year and I want to make a point of finishing the year how I started it - listening to what REAL Americans have to say. **This time, I 'm doing it over breakfast.**

I would like to invite you to enter for the opportunity to meet me for breakfast in New York City and talk about the direction of our country.

name: The White House
subject: Renewing a United Resolve
body:
1600 Daily
https://click.mail.whitehouse.gov/?qs=0b7e5400e0512b055c161d41730a95875bba4e209689bda79c4f629c849ce05d86deca056ecbf3835b58c349c12c0decdc79e49b1685989a

Vice President Mike Pence at the Wall Street Journal CEO Gathering

Yesterday, Vice President Mike Pence participated in the Wall Street Journal CEO Council gathering in Washington, D.C. The Vice President took questions regarding the Unified Framework for Tax Reform that is designed to ensure that all Americans are treated fairly, workers are able to keep more of their hard-earned money, and companies are incentivized to bring jobs back to the United States. By eliminating loopholes that disproportionately benefit the wealthy, promoting an attractive economic climate in the United States, and reducing the complexity of our current tax code, this “America First” tax reform plan will propel our Nation towards historic economic growth.

"This is a gathering of leaders of consequence. It’s amazing to think that companies gathered here represent $2.5 trillion in annual revenue and millions of jobs across the United States of America. So we thank you for your leadership. We thank you for your investment in America and your belief in America’s future. We know that communities large and small, across this country, depend on the companies that are represented here and the decisions you make each and every day." Vice President Pence said.

https://click.mail.whitehouse.gov/?qs=0b7e5400e0512b05c69e72b098560b411a6c78f11ab0faacaa5474e01c8c8563ed4b3790c350f5c16199ee66fcf2ecbb74317ed7f32b4250
Read the Vice President's full remarks and learn more about tax reform
https://click.mail.whitehouse.gov/?qs=0b7e5400e0512b05b817ed83554411bf36cc93ed02e1f12969a225fa2f612aeb3157f847a3a3afeab38b19f5d217e78b4cf9bd9bdfe964e8
here.

President Trump Returns from Asia

Last night, President Donald J. Trump arrived in the United States after concluding his longest foreign trip to date and the longest trip to Asia by an American president in more than a quarter century. President Trump utilized the journey as an opportunity to demonstrate the Administration’s commitment to the Indo-Pacific region, strengthen longstanding alliances, and establish new partnerships. The President visited Japan, China, Vietnam, and the Philippines, where he met with world leaders to discuss a range of strategic issues. President Trump was proud to advance American prosperity by strengthening international resolve to denuclearize North Korea, promoting a free and open Indo-Pacific region, and stimulating fair and reciprocal trade.

“People were taking advantage -- countries were taking advantage of the United States, more than just this region, but the world,” said President Trump. “Those days are over.”

See more of President Trump’s trip to Asia
https://click.mail.whitehouse.gov/?qs=0b7e5400e0512b05cf668c413a36fde176d43934884b602b5c9cd362e3781a8e7ff9ff61fdf798180e7d1319a8dcc032056f5119038eaa9f
here.

First Lady Melania Trump Visits Japan

First Lady Melania Trump joined her husband at Yokota Air Base in Japan early last week to thank members of the military for the sacrifices they have made to protect the United States. She then departed for Tokyo, where she joined Mrs. Akie Abe for tea and a cultural presentation on the history of the pearl industry. The First Lady also had the opportunity to travel to the Imperial Palace to meet with Emperor Akihito and Empress Michiko followed by an Honor Guard Ceremony at Akasaka Palace with Prime Minister and Mrs. Abe. First Lady Melania Trump and Mrs. Abe then traveled to Kyobashi Tsukiji Elementary School where they visited local school children and participated in a traditional calligraphy lesson.

“I enjoyed the time I was able to spend in Japan,” states First Lady Melania Trump. “It was a pleasure to once again spend time with Mrs. Abe, and discuss many of the issues facing each of our nations.”

Watch highlights from the First Lady’s trip to Japan
https://click.mail.whitehouse.gov/?qs=0b7e5400e0512b0582410651369d7c1d9ab7dbde507bfd7be14007632a44f8c65fc56ecacb8449d9d31b4ba3e6c7226bef5f1cd2acdf0bef
here.

Photo of the Day

President Donald J. Trump with President Rodrigo Duterte and Ms. Honeylet Avancena of the Philippines | November 13, 2017 (Official White House Photo by Shealah Craighead)

POTUS TODAY

Vice President Mike Pence and Second Lady Karen Pence will travel to Austin, Texas. There, the Vice President, joined by Governor Greg Abbott, Secretary of Energy Rick Perry, and FEMA Administrator Brock Long, will participate in a briefing on Hurricane Harvey relief and recovery efforts at the FEMA Joint Field Office.

COMING UP

Tomorrow, President Trump will deliver remarks on tax reform to the House Republican Conference at the United States Capitol. On Friday, the White House will welcome NCAA teams from across the country.

https://click.mail.whitehouse.gov/?qs=0b7e5400e0512b05a929a2a53f73648e2ce9c2be67c8af590ad02f8eb9f165dd15587089c9d6edf3a278614e757341cfb419556ece4d548f
Privacy Policy |

https://click.mail.whitehouse.gov/?qs=0b7e5400e0512b0560fcbce45b074b424891ee370a17f4ecb6381d990def28380752ad5ea1d761e9c75282930625d5ef89fae7f89cc8773c
Contact the White House |

https://click.mail.whitehouse.gov/unsub_center.aspx?qs=d453c42a6560d1d4dabaecb9a81d9f1c4f46c925e8a969143c8478614e52b48f0bb28f3a240fcea14107264565613fc5dc87dbb6e9764dc5

name: Jeanne Shaheen
subject: You're invited:
disclaimer_text: Paid for by Shaheen for Senate
body:
Friends,
The results from last week across New Hampshire, and the country, are clear - Democrats are energized! We are organized, and we are ready to win back seats across NH and the country!
From Berlin to Nashua, Democrats came out to vote and volunteer. More than 100 Democratic women won municipal elections across the state. With your help, we elected Joyce Craig to be the first woman to serve as mayor of Manchester. From Berlin to Rochester to Nashua and Keene, we saw Democrats stepping up all across the state, winning races up and down the ticket!
These achievements are remarkable. **[Something this big deserves a celebration. I hope you will join the New Hampshire Democratic Party at the second annual Kennedy Clinton Dinner this Friday, November 17, in Hollis.](http://www.democratsenators.org/dia/track.jsp?v=2&c=3ZazQ2Crez%2FvabH0whEg4kP%2FC4K0Wwq0&url=https%3A%2F%2Fsecure.actblue.com%2Fdonate%2Fkennedyclinton%3Frefcode%3Dcjs)**
We have a lot to celebrate, but our work is not done. The 2018 midterms are just around the corner, and there are elections coming up in communities across New Hampshire. The way we continue this momentum and build a stronger, more organized presence across the state is by continuing to show up to support grassroots efforts that make a difference.
**[Tickets are on sale now](http://www.democratsenators.org/dia/track.jsp?v=2&c=7UZ%2FG09waYDHeZJsTn%2FsaUP%2FC4K0Wwq0&url=https%3A%2F%2Fsecure.actblue.com%2Fdonate%2Fkennedyclinton%3Frefcode%3Dcjs)** , and you won't want to miss the dinner this Friday!
Warmest regards,
Jeanne
**[P.S. If you're unable to come, a donation of any amount will help get the NHDP ready for 2018.](http://www.democratsenators.org/dia/track.jsp?v=2&c=Juw5jXSDKdWmtV0Yym%2FRT0P%2FC4K0Wwq0&url=https%3A%2F%2Fsecure.actblue.com%2Fdonate%2Fnhmomentum%3Frefcode%3Dcjs%26amount%3D25.00)**

body:

A pro-coal panel led by fossil fuel promoters. At a _climate change_ conference. So we showed up to keep them honest. Oregon Gov. Kate Brown and I brought a clear message to the press at their panel: While Trump denies climate change, we are moving forward to a clean energy future. On the West Coast, we're building a Great Blue Wall that puts people and our climate ahead of the big-coal and big-oil lobby. [**Join us: Add your name to speak out against Donald Trump 's denial of climate science.**](http://links.e.jayinslee.com/ctt?kn=2&ms=MTIzNjc2MTgS1&r=MTM2NjM5NDY2ODI1S0&b=0&j=MTI4MTQzMTk3NAS2&mt=1&rt=0) [](http://links.e.jayinslee.com/ctt?kn=2&ms=MTIzNjc2MTgS1&r=MTM2NjM5NDY2ODI1S0&b=0&j=MTI4MTQzMTk3NAS2&mt=1&rt=0) Every single person I talked to in Bonn was more energized than ever to take on climate action. It's a shame that the White House isn't harnessing that energy -- but where they fall short in leadership, we'll stand up to prove our strength. When confronted with anti-science lies that threaten our world's future, it's our moral responsibility to speak the truth, loudly and forcefully. And it's our responsibility to act. That's what we did in Bonn. That's what we're doing in Washington state -- and across the country with the U.S. Climate Alliance, a partnership with 14 of my fellow governors collectively representing almost 40% of the U.S. economy. name: Jason Yates, My Faith Votes
subject: November 7 Election Results Rundown
body:
| |
---
[ ](http://www2.myfaithvotes.org/r?u=http%3A%2F%2Fwww.myfaithvotes.org%2F2017-election-results-rundown%2F&e=be53806c85fc17bd685e1e4c4d896e92&utm_source=myfaithvotes&utm_medium=email&utm_campaign=171115_err_s1&n=1)
| While many people referred to 2017 as an “off-year” for elections, last week more than 2,000 elections took place. Forty percent of the nation had an opportunity to cast a vote last week. Here is a quick rundown of the outcomes and our observations of some of those key elections:
# Competitive School Board Races
92 percent of candidates in nearly 1,000 school board elections across the nation faced opposition in last week’s elections.That’s an increase in contested races of about 18 percent from last year’s November elections, according to Ballotpedia. One school board race we watched closely was in Douglas County, Colorado. Central to the election was the issue of school vouchers. Given that 4 of the board’s 7 seats were up for election, the outcome would establish the majority for the board, and therefore, the agenda for the school district over next four years. Central to the future agenda of the board is the opportunity to challenge – or not – the state constitution prohibiting parents from directing state-provided funds to private religious schools. All four of the seats were won by candidates who oppose the voucher program, and therefore, it can be assumed that the board will decide not to pursue further action on the voucher program. Even while the election received national attention and funding, voter turnout was no higher than that of prior years.
* * *
# Socially Progressive Agenda Won in State Races
Many are talking about the races for governor in New Jersey and Virginia as a referendum on Donald Trump. That may be, but either way, the outcomes were an advancement for a liberal agenda. The elected governors, Phil Murphy (NJ) and Ralph Northam (VA), promoted progressive agendas in their election bids, supporting the protection of abortion rights, prohibiting or limiting parental school choice for their children, and elevating sexual orientation and gender identity to a protected class, to name a few. In both states, Democrats now control the executive and legislative branches of the state government. Six other states are also fully controlled by Democrats. 26 states are fully controlled by Republicans.
* * *
# High Success Rates of Incumbents
In the over 300 municipal races with an incumbent candidate, the incumbent won in 90% of the elections. Most of these wins were in large, liberally-progressive cities, such as New York, Minneapolis, Detroit, and Boston.
* * *
# Democrats are Motivated
All eyes were on Virginia, a purple state, to see if voters would turn out in large enough numbers as a repudiation of Donald Trump. It was the highest voter turnout in 20 years in the Virginia governor’s race. While Republicans increased voter turnout by 10% from 4 years prior, Democrats increased their turnout by 30%. The result: Virginia Governor-elect Ralph Northam, a Democrat, beat his Republican opponent Ed Gillespie by eight percentage points, a larger margin than predicted in polls leading up to the election. Democrats are motivated by this outcome and are now looking to mirror the strategies used in Virginia in key states for the 2018 midterms. It is predicted that next year’s elections will set record levels for campaign spending by candidates and special interest groups motivated to establish control in Congress.
* * *
# Utah’s 3rd Congressional District Seat
A special election was held in the 3rd Congressional District of Utah for the U.S. House of Representatives. Provo Mayor John Curtis (R) defeated Kathie Allen (D) in the election to replace Jason Chaffetz (R), who resigned from office on June 30, 2017. The Republican win was no surprise, as the district was ranked by The Cook Political Report as the 16th most Republican congressional district in the country. The win gives Republicans 241 seats in the House compared to the 194 seats held by Democrats. Because this was a special election for Utah’s 3rd District, Curtis’ seat will be up for election again next year when all 435 seats in the House of Representatives will be voted upon.
* * *

name: Joe Donnelly
subject: It's hard to believe
disclaimer_text: Paid for by Donnelly for Indiana
body:
As I travel around the state talking to Hoosiers, I am constantly reminded of how important the Great Lakes are to Indiana. |
---

| | Peter,

As I travel around the state talking to Hoosiers, I am constantly reminded of how important the Great Lakes are to Indiana. Thousands of families take their children and grandchildren to Lake Michigan every year to vacation together. Jill and I have done the same with our kids.

The Lakes are one of our nation's most beautiful natural resources, and I have great hope that my grandchildren and their children will enjoy the lake just as generations of Hoosier families have over the years. **But, Peter, the Great Lakes are much more than a place to get away from it all -- they're critical to some of our state's most important industries and they provide access to safe drinking water to more than 40 million people across our region.**

That's why it's hard to believe that the White House's budget request proposed eliminating our best tool to protect the Great Lakes, the Great Lakes Restoration Initiative (GLRI).

**Will you chip in today to make sure we can keep fighting to protect the Great Lakes and our economy?** | _If you've saved your payment information with ActBlue Express, your donation will go through immediately:_

The special interests behind this Administration's push to gut the GLRI aren't concerned with making sure our children can appreciate the beauty of the lakes. They're also looking past the damage this will do to our local economy and our health. Because this isn't just about conservation: **Defunding the GLRI would cost Indiana over $16 BILLION in economic activity, 100,000 jobs, and could put our state's drinking water at risk.**

We have to protect the Great Lakes with everything we've got. That's why I need you to have my back now, Peter. **[Will you chip in $5 to make sure we can keep fighting to protect the Great Lakes?](http://action.joeforindiana.com/page/m/7f7c2541/7377f838/356dd629/51fba4f1/2807363578/VEsA/ "https://secure.actblue.com/donate/jd-greatlakesnov?refcode=20171115nd&amount=10")**

Joe

subject: Re: Al Franken's tattoo
disclaimer_text: Paid for by the DSCC, dscc
body:
With TEN Democratic women Senators up for re-election right now, it's important that we step up to support the Women's Senate Network.
---
Wow. The response to Al Franken's message this week has been incredible.

Grassroots donors are stepping up to support the Women's Senate Network because they know the importance of re-electing our TEN female Senators on the ballot in 2018. Will you join them?

[**Chip in $1 to the Women's Senate Network today to support our TEN Democratic women Senators facing re-election.**](http://action.dscc. "https://secure.actblue.com/donate/wsn-2018?refcode=DPE-20171115-Myers-ProsHV&recurring=auto&amounts=5,10,25,50,100,150,250&amount=5&firstname=&lastname=&email=dpwillis67@gmail.com&addr1= &city=&state=&zip=&phone=&employer=&occupation=")

Al Franken even said he'd be willing to get a Women's Senate Network tattoo...but I think the message below will do the trick.

Thanks for your support,

Mindy

\------Forwarded Message------

From: Al Franken
Date: Monday, November 13, 2017
Subject: I told them I'd do anything

[](http://action.dscc. "https://secure.actblue.com/donate/wsn-2018?refcode=DPE-20171115-Myers-ProsHV&recurring=auto&amounts=5,10,25,50,100,150,250&amount=5&firstname=&lastname=&email=dpwillis67@gmail.com&addr1= &city=&state=&zip=&phone=&employer=&occupation=")
Dear team,

When the DSCC asked me to help out with the **Women 's Senate Network**, I told them I'd do anything. Knock on doors. Stuff envelopes. Get a huge "Women's Senate Network" tattoo.

"Um, how about let's start with a fundraising email?" they suggested. Good idea.

**[Please click here to make a contribution to the Women's Senate Network to support the TEN Democratic women senators on the ballot in 2018!](http://action.dscc. "https://secure.actblue.com/donate/wsn-2018?refcode=DPE-20171115-Myers-ProsHV&recurring=auto&amounts=5,10,25,50,100,150,250&amount=5&firstname=&lastname=&email=dpwillis67@gmail.com&addr1= &city=&state=&zip=&phone=&employer=&occupation=")**

Now, if you are a keen political observer, you will notice that I am not, myself, a woman. So why is the Women's Senate Network so important to me? Three reasons.

1. The ten Democratic women up for re-election next year represent nearly half of all women in the Senate. And the last thing we need around here is to have fewer women in positions of power.

2. The Republican plan for growing their majority and ramming through more of President Trump's far-right agenda revolves around taking out as many of these women as they can -- in fact, **several are already facing attack ads.**

3. These women aren't just Democratic votes -- they're Democratic leaders, including dynamic progressive champions like **Elizabeth Warren** and **Tammy Baldwin** , tough-as-nails fighters like **Claire McCaskill** and **Mazie Hirono** , and even **Amy Klobuchar** : my senior senator, my role model, and one of the smartest, hardest-working, and (okay, I'll admit it) funniest people in Washington.

**Imagine what the Senate would be like without these women, and you 'll understand why we can't afford to lose any of these incredible leaders.**

[**Will you help me help them by making a contribution to support the Women 's Senate Network today?**](http://action.dscc. "https://secure.actblue.com/donate/wsn-2018?refcode=DPE-20171115-Myers-ProsHV&recurring=auto&amounts=5,10,25,50,100,150,250&amount=5&firstname=&lastname=&email=dpwillis67@gmail.com&addr1= &city=&state=&zip=&phone=&employer=&occupation=")

Look, I know all about tough races. But there's no denying that the other side makes it even harder on women who dare to step up and run. Those who do it anyway -- and win -- often wind up being some of our most indispensable leaders: tough, smart, courageous Democrats who never back down from a fight.

It's also why it's so important that we stand with them right now. We've never had an election where so many Democratic women are under attack -- and the truth is, 2018 is shaping up to be an even tougher-than-usual year to be on the ballot.

So I hope you'll join me in support of the Women's Senate Network by [**clicking here and making a contribution right now**](http://action.dscc. "https://secure.actblue.com/donate/wsn-2018?refcode=DPE-20171115-Myers-ProsHV&recurring=auto&amounts=5,10,25,50,100,150,250&amount=5&firstname=&lastname=&email=dpwillis67@gmail.com&addr1= &city=&state=&zip=&phone=&employer=&occupation="). Especially because Franni tells me the tattoo idea is a non-starter.

Thanks,
Al

P.S.: I'm going to do everything I can to stand with these ten awesome women - and I hope you are, too. A great way to start would be to [**click here and help support the Women 's Senate Network today!**](http://action.dscc. "https://secure.actblue.com/donate/wsn-2018?refcode=DPE-20171115-Myers-ProsHV&recurring=auto&amounts=5,10,25,50,100,150,250&amount=5&firstname=&lastname=&email=dpwillis67@gmail.com&addr1= &city=&state=&zip=&phone=&employer=&occupation=")

120 Maryland Ave. NE
Washington, DC 20002** Paid for by the DSCC, dscc.org,
and not authorized by any candidate or candidate's committee.
The DSCC is the only committee solely dedicated to supporting Democrats in the U.S. Senate. This message was sent to dpwillis67@gmail.com because you are a part of the DSCC's grassroots network. Please add us to your address book or approved senders list to make sure you keep receiving DSCC updates. Emails are a critical way for us to stay in touch with our grassroots supporters. 99% of the DSCC's contributions come from grassroots donors giving less than $200. Imagine what the Senate would be like without these women, and you 'll understand why we can't afford to lose any of these incredible leaders. It's also why it's so important that we stand with them right now. We've never had an election where so many Democratic women are under attack -- and the truth is, 2018 is shaping up to be an even tougher-than-usual year to be on the ballot. name: Michael Lighty, The Sanders Institute
subject: New Medicaid Work Requirements Will Deny More Care
disclaimer_text: PAID FOR BY THE SANDERS INSTITUTE
body:
#
Friend,
Every day seems to bring another way for the current administration to deny healthcare to people in need. The latest policy shift toward "less is more" care involves applying a work requirement to Medicaid recipients. The CMA Administrator, Seema Verma, has proposed that in order to receive Medicaid benefits, those able must do "community engagement." That's policy-speak for forcing someone to work--possibly unpaid--or lose their healthcare coverage. Ignoring the evidence that 59% of able-bodied people on Medicaid already work, this looks like a so-called solution to a non-problem.
We cannot let our outrage fade away under the daily onslaught of provocative policy proposals. As a Fellow of the Sanders Institute and the Director of Public Policy at National Nurses United, I feel it is important to highlight this issue and challenge the conservative dogma that would deny people the healthcare they need simply because they do not fulfill an onerous and unrelated work requirement.
Medicaid provides a safety net to the most vulnerable and poor in our society. Imposing new barriers to care is inhuman, and reflects not "hope" as Administrator Verma professes, but cynicism: it scapegoats low-income people to save money for state and federal budgets.
[**_New Medicaid Work Requirements Will Deny More Care_**](https://sandersinstitute-ourrev.bsd.net/page/m/2ba7ae67/bed43eb/2e49b0ce/75ff571f/4138254288/VEsH/ "https://www.sandersinstitute.com/blog/new-medicaid-work-requirements-deny-more-care-?source=em171115")
In the fight over ACA repeal, Medicaid has achieved a new-found popularity. If the Administration wants to use Medicaid as a social engineering experiment to punish the poor, it's up to us to stop them. Again.
Thank you for staying engaged,
Michael Lighty
Fellow, The Sanders Institute

name: Andrew Gillum
subject: No surprise
body:
#
Hello --

After 20 years of Republicans in the Governor's Mansion, Floridians have seen budget after budget pass that overwhelmingly help the wealthiest few and hurt working families.

As Governor Scott closes out his second term and prepares to run for Senate, he's desperately trying to cover up seven years of failed policies, and his $87.4 billion proposal devoted to "fighting for Florida's families" isn't fooling anyone.

No eleventh-hour budget proposal can cover up the facts that nearly half of our state's households are struggling to make ends meet and many of our rural counties have lost jobs since 2007.

**[Under Governor Scott, working families have suffered, and we cannot afford another four years of the same unfair budgets. So chip in $5 now and help elect a Governor who will put all Floridians' well-being before failed politics.](http://act.andrewgillum.com/page/m/fcb3242/9e00690/4e88c034/24030418/43551670/VEsH/ "https://secure.actblue.com/donate/gillum-scottbudget?utm_campaign=em17_171115_budget_fnd_gq_ndn__AG&utm_medium=email&utm_source=Gillum&refcode=em17_171115_budget_fnd_gq_ndn__AG&utm_content=1+-+Under+Governor+Scott+working+families+ha")**

Governor Scott's budgets have always reflected the same self-serving politics that have defined his career: slashing investment in our public schools, over-promising and under-delivering for our environment and doing far too little on health care -- all while giving handouts to his well-off and well-connected donors and friends.

**Budgets reflect our values, and for seven years we've seen just what Governor Scott's are: cuts to programs that are critical for working families.**

As Governor, I'm going to put working families first from Day One with higher wages, including paying teachers what they are worth, and expanding access to affordable health care, but I need your help to get there.

**[Chip in $5 or more now and let's change the status quo together.](http://act.andrewgillum.com/page/m/fcb3242/9e00690/4e88c034/2403041b/43551670/VEsE/ "https://secure.actblue.com/donate/gillum-scottbudget?utm_campaign=em17_171115_budget_fnd_gq_ndn__AG&utm_medium=email&utm_source=Gillum&refcode=em17_171115_budget_fnd_gq_ndn__AG&utm_content=2+-+Chip+in+5+or+more+now+and+lets+change+th")**

Thank you,

Andrew
